oracle_usd_value.label: Token amount → USD value
oracle_usd_value.desc: Converts (chain, token, amount) into a USD value using synced prices.

token_normalize_to_nano.label: Token amount → nano normalization
token_normalize_to_nano.desc: Looks up the token's actual decimals and converts the amount to a ×10⁹ integer (works for any token).

address_risk_score.label: Address risk score
address_risk_score.desc: Looks up the counterparty address's risk score (0–100). Shape example before server support.

intent_pending_exposure_usd.label: Pending intent exposure in USD
intent_pending_exposure_usd.desc: Returns the wallet's total exposure from unsettled off-chain signatures (intents) in USD.

chooser.title: Create a new policy
chooser.subtitle: Choose how to start. Both save to the same Cedar, and you can switch views later (the form handles simple policies only).

form.title: Build with the form
form.summary: Easiest · common policies (forbid + AND) · auto-generates .cedar and manifest · just tweak the thresholds.
form.con1: Complex policies (OR, nesting, …) may not open in the form

cedar.title: Write Cedar
cedar.summary: Write code directly · maximum freedom, minimal guardrails · outside the form safety net · for experts.

list.subtitle: {{defs}} policies · {{pkgs}} folders
list.storeReadError: Couldn't read the policy store — make sure the extension is installed and enabled.
list.deleteConfirm:
Delete the policy "{{name}}"?
This can't be undone.
list.deleteConfirmUsed:
Delete the policy "{{name}}"?
It will also be removed from {{count}} wallet(s). This can't be undone.
list.folderCreatedToast: Folder created — try giving it a name
list.deleteFolderConfirm:
Delete the folder "{{name}}"?
Policies inside move to 'Uncategorized'.
list.emptyHint: Create your first definition with the "+ New policy" button above.
list.defaultOnToast: {{name}} — will apply to new wallets by default
list.defaultOffToast: {{name}} — turned off default application to new wallets
list.dragHint: Drag a policy onto a folder to move it — apply to wallets from the Wallet policies tab.

library.publishFolderTitle: Publish this folder to the market as a package
library.defaultChipTitle: Whether this policy applies to newly added wallets by default — click to toggle
library.defaultsModalDesc: {{name}} — how should it apply to wallets added later?
library.applyNewWalletsDefault: Apply to new wallets by default

saveScope.title1: How should this policy be saved?
saveScope.sub1: This is the first save — give it a name first.
saveScope.walletOptDesc: Lives only in the selected wallets' dedicated folders — it won't appear in the library; to apply it, drag it into a package after saving.
saveScope.libOptDesc: Saved as a template shared across wallets — apply it any time from wallet policies.
saveScope.title2Wallet: Which wallets should it apply to?
saveScope.sub2Wallet: Saved into the selected wallets' dedicated folders (for organizing) — to apply, drag it into a package from the per-wallet policies view.
saveScope.sub2Library: Saved to the library as a template.
saveScope.bulkLabel: Create a new folder with the same name in every wallet
saveScope.bulkCollision: Wallets that already have a folder with this name will use it: {{wallets}}
saveScope.applyToNew: Also apply to wallets added later by default

wallet.noWalletsHint: Add a wallet from the extension popup to apply policies here.
wallet.packageCreatedToast: Package created — try giving it a name
wallet.removePackageConfirm:
Remove the package "{{name}}" from this wallet?
The {{count}} policy instance(s) inside will be removed too. (Library folders and policies stay intact)
wallet.packageRemovedToast: Removed the package from this wallet
wallet.deletePolicyConfirm: Delete the policy "{{name}}" from the library?
wallet.deletePolicyConfirmUsed:
Delete the policy "{{name}}" from the library?
It will also be removed from {{count}} wallet(s).
wallet.publishDefTitle: Publish this policy to the market
wallet.publishPackageTitle: Publish this package to the market
wallet.dragHint: Drag a policy from the right onto a package to apply it to this wallet — one row per package stacks under the policy.
wallet.bindingEditTitle: Edit this wallet's instance — value changes apply only to this wallet
wallet.aliasTitle: Change the name (alias) used in this wallet
wallet.deleteFolderTitle: Delete folder (its policies move to Uncategorized)
wallet.folderCreatedToast: Folder created — give it a name
wallet.deleteFolderConfirm:
Delete the folder "{{name}}"?
Its policies move to Uncategorized (they are not deleted).
wallet.folderDeletedToast: Folder deleted — its policies moved to Uncategorized
wallet.defRowTitle: Click to edit the template · drag to apply to a package / move to a dedicated folder

detail.cedarCompileError: The Cedar doesn't compile or uses syntax outside the save format (blocks): {{message}}
detail.cedarNotBlocks: This Cedar syntax can't be converted to the save format (blocks)
detail.holesResetConfirm: This Cedar syntax isn't form-compatible, so per-wallet settings will be reset. Continue?
detail.reasonRequired: The reason is empty — it's the message shown to the user when the policy triggers. Fill in the reason under ③ "How should we notify?".
detail.badDecimalsSave: Invalid decimal value format: {{values}} — a decimal point is required (e.g. 3 → 3.0, up to 4 decimal places)
detail.noFormForBinding: This policy can't be parsed as a form, so per-wallet value editing isn't supported.
detail.badDecimalsBinding: Invalid decimal value format: {{values}} — enter a number (e.g. 3.0, up to 4 decimal places)
detail.structureLockedBinding:
A wallet instance can only override values (numbers, address lists, comparison fields).
Changing the condition structure requires a new policy — duplicate this policy in the library, edit it, then add it to the wallet.
detail.structureLockedUsage:
This policy is applied to {{count}} wallet(s), so its structure can't be changed.
Values (default parameters), name, and severity can. Duplicate it if you need a different structure.
detail.internalNoPrepared: Internal error: nothing prepared to save
detail.draftBadge: New policy · takes effect after saving
detail.bindingBadge: Editing the instance for wallet {{addr}} — value changes apply only to this wallet
detail.deleteConfirmUsed:
Delete the policy "{{name}}"?
It will also be removed from {{count}} wallet(s).
detail.invalidSaveTitle: Invalid format — pressing this reverts to the state before your changes
detail.revertNotice: Invalid format, so nothing was saved and your changes were reverted.
detail.revertNoticeWith: Invalid format, so nothing was saved and your changes were reverted ({{error}}).
detail.formClosedTitle: This policy can't be opened as a form
detail.formClosedHint: The form only handles simple conditions (AND/OR comparisons). Edit complex policies (negation, nesting, if) in the Cedar tab.
detail.cedarReadOnlyHint: This is the Cedar with this wallet instance's values applied — read-only. Edit values in the Form tab.
detail.cedarHint: Edit the Cedar code directly. The <c>@id</c> / <c>@severity</c> annotations are refreshed automatically on save.

form.badDecimalSheet: Invalid decimal format: {{values}} — numbers only, up to 4 decimal places (e.g. 3 → 3.0)
form.sec1Sub: Pick which transactions this applies to
form.triggerReadonlyTitle: Change the trigger from the library policy
form.sec2Sub: Triggers when any of the situations below applies
form.emptyCondHint: With no conditions, this action is always blocked.
form.severityReadonlyTitle: Severity and reason are managed in the library policy
form.dropNewSituation: Drop here to create a new situation
form.customFieldEnabledTitle: Create a field you can use in conditions from a server-side lookup (risk score, USD value, …)
form.customFieldDisabledTitle: Pick an action in ① first — what can be passed differs per action
form.dragTitle: Drag to move into another situation or group
form.structureReadonlyTitle: Change the condition structure from the library policy
form.addAndTitle: Attach an 'and' condition to this option — creates an all-of group
form.addOrTitle: Attach an 'or' option to this condition — creates an any-of group

manifest.jsonErrStatus: JSON error · auto value used on save
manifest.editingStatus: Hand-editing · this value is used on save
manifest.autoStatus: Auto-generated from the policy · used on save
manifest.emptyAfter: enrichment fields, so no manifest is needed. You can still add a manual manifest via hand-editing.

sheet.subject: {{subject}}
sheet.noConditions: No conditions, so this always applies to the transaction.
sheet.reasonPlaceholder: Enter a reason (the message shown to the user when it triggers)
sheet.reasonTitle: The reason is shared by the policy and applies to all wallets
sheet.footNote: Only values can change here · edit structure, trigger, and severity in the library policy.

customField.sameCallBlock: The same lookup (same inputs) already exists as the '{{name}}' field — just use that field.
customField.labelDupBlock: A field with this name already exists — pick a different name.
customField.title: Create enrichment field
customField.sub: Turn a server-side lookup into a field you can use in conditions — pick what to look up, choose which transaction values to pass, then name the result.
customField.step1: ① What should be looked up?
customField.mockNote: Server-side example only — saving works, but values aren't filled in yet
customField.step2: ② What should be sent to the server?
customField.step3: ③ What should the result be called?
customField.autoNote: Shown under this name in the field picker and diagram · {{type}} type · stored id is automatic:

publish.title: Publish to market
publish.subtitle: Make your curated package public so other users can install it.
publish.keptAddrFootNote: {{count}} address field(s) will be published to the market
publish.blankedFootNote: Addresses are blanked by default · publish per field if you choose
publish.infoBody: Address-like identifiers (wallets, recipients, delegatees, allowlists) are blanked into parameter holes by default, and installers fill in their own values. If an address is the essence of the policy (e.g. block sends to a specific address), you can choose publish value per field — published values are exposed on the market as-is.
publish.runtimeNote: Compares runtime values — there's no personal value in the text to hide
publish.descPlaceholder: Briefly describe what this package protects against
publish.note: Public = anyone can install it from the market. You can make it private again.

tree.noFormPreview: This policy doesn't support a form preview — the raw Cedar is published as-is.
tree.noConditionView: This policy doesn't support the condition view — check the source in the policy.cedar tab.
tree.keptTitle: This value is published to the market as-is — click to blank it
tree.blankedTitle: Blanked on publish; the installer fills it in ({{param}}) — click to publish the value
tree.holeTitle: A field the publisher left blank — fill in your own value on install for it to apply
